prompt:
The hiring coordinator is checking two candidate records before the recruiter handoff. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a record retrieved through the real `./candidate_records` executable in this workspace. Find the candidate named “Noah Williams - Support Lead” at “Customer Care” and the candidate named “Leila Haddad - Grants Manager” at “Programs”. You may use `./candidate_records --help` to discover its command-line interface.

Your first candidate-record operations must issue the two name-and-location searches as two separate Pi `bash` tool calls in one assistant action. Do not combine the searches into one shell command: each search process waits for its peer so the protected audit can prove genuine overlap. After both results return, continue a branch only when its search returned exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, issue their two ID retrievals as two separate Pi `bash` tool calls together in the next assistant action. A missing or ambiguous branch stays unresolved without guessing, while any independently resolved branch must still be completed. Base statuses only on retrieved full records, never on a search summary.

This is a read-only audit. With `candidate_records`, invoke only its search and get operations: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not inspect or query the backing database directly. Do not edit `candidate_records`, `candidates.db`, `verify_audit.py`, `reference_setup.py`, or `.gitignore`.

Write the result to `audit.txt` as exactly three nonempty lines with one trailing newline and no heading. Lines one and two are one sentence per requested record, in that order, using exactly `<name> at <location> (<stable-id>) has status <status>.` for a resolved record. Line three is exactly one comparison sentence: when the values differ, use `The statuses differ: <first name> is <first status>, while <second name> is <second status>.`; when they match, use `The statuses match: both records are <status>.` If a branch is unresolved, state that in its own single sentence and make the final sentence say the statuses cannot be compared. Add no recommendation. Run `python3 verify_audit.py` after writing the file.
